chunks.text: The expense of urban housing rises year after year.
Many young people cannot even afford the rent and continue living with their parents.
Taking out a mortgage to buy a home is no longer the obvious choice it once was.
Real estate prices have been increasing at a pace that far exceeds the growth of earnings.
This issue has triggered intense debate as a domestic policy matter in many countries.
The mayor and governor propose measures, but their effect remains limited.
Numerous resident have been moving to cheaper suburb.
But commuting times grow considerably longer
and a decline in the quality of life is an outcome that cannot be ignored.
The housing problem is not merely an economic question.
A stable home is directly connected to children's education, the health of residents
and the stability of a community.
Adequate housing should be the right of every person.
